LAW AND MOTION, 302, MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF GILBERT CHAN ADEQUATELY ALLEGES PUNITIVE DAMAGES LIABILITY ON THE PART OF MR. DAILLEY. ATTEMPTING TO FLEE THE SCENE AFTER "RAMMING" A PEDESTRIAN WITH A VEHICLE IS DESPICABLE CONDUCT THAT IS IN WILLFUL AND CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE PEDESTRIAN'S RIGHTS. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed. The Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court 3.1385. Judge: Teri L. Jackson; Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-23-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-06-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-15-2017 Set For Trial On Nov-27-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-21-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, John P. Strouss III of the law offices of John P. Strouss III (415.771.6174) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Edgar W. Hawkyard of the law offices of Catherine A. Walsh (925.825.9500) appears via telephone as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT RYAN DAILLEY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, as to the punitive damages allegations in the complaint is DENIED. Plaintiff Gilbert Chan adequately alleges punitive damages liability on the part of Mr. Dailley. Attempting to flee the scene after "ramming" a pedestrian with a vehicle is despicable conduct that is in willful and conscious disregard of the pedestrian's rights. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
